scene_summary: A minimal ceramic plate: three pastel-glazed vessels on a white plinth, dominated by a cropped blue storm-belly vessel at right. Source-bearing anchors appear as real apertures, rim shadows, glaze chips, flecks, drip scars, and a broken plinth edge rather than interface cards.
usable_surfaces: large blue storm-belly aperture and weathered glaze
rear peach vessel rim shadow and side pinhole
front round vessel dark mouth and blue-orange glaze field
branching pale crack near lower right belly
small diamond glaze chip on large vessel
milky reflection slash on rear vessel
torn plinth side cut and chipped front edge
tiny pinholes and flecks embedded in ceramic skin

detected_objects.label: giant storm-belly aperture
detected_objects.visual_evidence: dark teal circular opening set into the upper right blue vessel, with a bright worn rim and visible inner depth

detected_objects.label: front lightning seam chip
detected_objects.visual_evidence: thin pale branching crack and chip scar descending across the lower right belly, with a horizontal scrape crossing the seam

detected_objects.label: studio-plinth side cut
detected_objects.visual_evidence: large torn V-shaped incision in the front face of the white plinth directly below the vessels

detected_objects.label: catalog-row glaze rupture
detected_objects.visual_evidence: front round vessel reads as a broad cup-like glaze field with repeated mottled blue, peach, and yellow ceramic rhythms across its belly

complexity_assessment.rationale: The plate is sparse and expressionist: three vessel masses, one plinth plane, and a handful of tight marks. The source windows have clear ceramic anchors without clutter.

mapping_note: Package assembly filled unmapped source pockets from planned artifact surfaces because plate inspection returned too few detected objects.
